jobs.title: Security Operations Analyst
jobs.description_short: Live monitoring, incident coordination, and reporting from the GTS Command Centre in Nairobi.
jobs.summary: We are hiring a Security Operations Analyst to work in the GTS Command Centre, supporting live monitoring of client sites, incident coordination, and structured reporting. The role is well-suited to security or intelligence practitioners with two to four years of relevant experience who want to operate inside a senior practitioner culture.
responsibilities: Monitor live security feeds, alarm systems, and intelligence inputs from client environments
Coordinate field response to incidents in line with documented escalation protocols
Maintain accurate incident logs and structured shift handover documentation
Provide real-time situational updates to clients and internal leadership
Support drill, exercise, and after-action review activity
Contribute to continuous improvement of command-centre procedures and tooling
requirements: Two to four years of experience in security operations, intelligence, or related field
Strong written and verbal communication in English; Kiswahili an advantage
Comfort working rotating shift patterns (including nights and weekends)
Familiarity with CCTV systems, access control platforms, and incident management tools
High personal standards around discretion, accuracy, and operational reliability

jobs.title: Senior Risk Consultant
jobs.description_short: Lead security and risk consulting engagements across East Africa for corporate, NGO, and government clients.
jobs.summary: We are hiring a Senior Risk Consultant to lead security and risk consulting engagements across East Africa — assessments, audits, M&A diligence, market entry analysis, and embedded advisory work. Suited to an experienced consultant or in-house risk leader ready to operate at engagement-partner level in a senior practitioner environment.
responsibilities: Lead end-to-end delivery of security risk assessments, control audits, and advisory engagements
Manage client relationships through scoping, fieldwork, reporting, and follow-through
Author analytically rigorous, decision-ready reports for boards and executive leadership
Mentor associates and analysts across engagement workstreams
Contribute to business development and proposal work
Represent GTS in client briefings and selected external forums
requirements: Eight or more years of experience in security risk consulting, in-house risk leadership, or related field
Demonstrated experience leading multi-disciplinary risk engagements across multiple African markets
Strong analytical writing — capable of producing board-grade output
Comfort engaging at C-suite and board level
Track record of building client relationships that endure beyond a single engagement
Willingness to travel across the region — typically 30–40% of working time

jobs.title: Intelligence Researcher
jobs.description_short: Open-source research and analysis supporting GTS country risk, daily monitoring, and bespoke intelligence products.
jobs.summary: We are hiring an Intelligence Researcher to join the GTS Intelligence Desk in Nairobi — supporting country risk analysis, daily monitoring briefs, and bespoke intelligence reports for corporate, NGO, and investor clients. Strong written analytical skills are essential.
responsibilities: Conduct structured open-source research across political, security, and economic topics
Contribute to country risk analyses and bespoke intelligence products
Author sections of daily monitoring briefs to a high editorial standard
Maintain country-level intelligence trackers and supporting reference material
Support the maintenance and quarterly updates of the GTS Risk Heat Map
Engage with field sources and partners under analyst-team supervision
requirements: Postgraduate degree in international relations, security studies, area studies, or related discipline (preferred)
One to three years of experience in analytical research or journalism
Demonstrated ability to write clear, analytically sharp prose under time pressure
Working knowledge of African political and security dynamics — regional depth in any sub-region a strong advantage
Languages beyond English (French, Arabic, Portuguese) a significant advantage
Genuine intellectual curiosity and discipline around evidence and sourcing
